title: Houses for rent in East Cleveland, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in East Cleveland, OH
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in East Cleveland, OH? See rent ranges, RTA Red Line & HealthLine access, and Section 8 facts, then contact us about nearby homes.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent East Cleveland ohio
answerBox: East Cleveland is a small, independent Cuyahoga County city bordering Cleveland's University Circle area, served by the RTA Red Line's Windermere station and the HealthLine bus rapid transit route on Euclid Avenue. Renters can expect rents generally in line with nearby Cleveland neighborhoods; contact us to ask about voucher-friendly homes we work with close by.

sections.h2: Renting a house in East Cleveland
body: East Cleveland is its own incorporated city in Cuyahoga County — not a Cleveland neighborhood — sitting directly east of Cleveland's University Circle area, home to Case Western Reserve University, the Cleveland Museum of Art, and the Cleveland Clinic's main campus corridor. Much of the housing stock is early-to-mid twentieth-century single-family homes, duplexes, and small apartment buildings typical of Cleveland's inner-ring East Side suburbs.

The city is small in area but sits at an important crossroads: Euclid Avenue (also carrying the HealthLine) runs straight through it connecting University Circle to the eastern suburbs, and its Terrace Road and Hayden Avenue corridors are lined with the kind of older two- and three-family homes common throughout Cleveland's East Side. Renters searching East Cleveland listings often also search Cleveland's adjoining Glenville and Hough neighborhoods, since all three share a similar housing stock and price range.

Our selection is currently concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, including **Glenville** and **Collinwood**, both a short drive from East Cleveland — we don't have confirmed inventory inside East Cleveland itself today. If East Cleveland is your target area, [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we'll follow up about any matching, voucher-friendly homes nearby, including [Glenville rentals](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-glenville-cleveland/), or browse our full list of [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/).

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent a house near East Cleveland?
body: Dedicated East Cleveland rent data isn't published separately, so the closest reliable benchmark is the wider Cleveland-area market. Zumper's July 4, 2026 report put the area's median asking rent at $1,250 a month, up 4.2% year-over-year, with two-bedroom homes around $1,100 and three-bedroom homes around $1,350. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which tracks larger professionally-managed apartment communities, found a higher average of $1,564 a month for 787 square feet. An individual East Cleveland house's actual asking rent will depend on its size, age, and condition — contact us and we can point you toward comparable homes we work with in nearby Cleveland neighborhoods.

East Cleveland sits at the eastern end of the RTA Red Line, whose Windermere station terminates in the city — the only Red Line stop actually located in East Cleveland, with a direct one-seat ride to Tower City downtown and on to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport. The HealthLine, GCRTA's bus rapid transit route along Euclid Avenue, also connects East Cleveland to University Circle and Public Square via a dedicated median busway, so renters without a car can reach downtown, University Circle's hospitals and museums, and the airport without transferring.

items: **Rail:** Red Line Windermere station (eastern terminus) — direct to Tower City and Cleveland Hopkins Airport
**Bus rapid transit:** HealthLine on Euclid Avenue to University Circle and Public Square
**Location:** Cuyahoga County, bordering Cleveland's University Circle area

sections.h2: Section 8 and housing vouchers in East Cleveland
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8), and as of July 2026 East Cleveland is not among the handful of Cuyahoga County communities that have passed their own source-of-income protection ordinances — that shorter list currently includes Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale. That means a market-rate landlord in East Cleveland can lawfully decline a voucher, so it's worth confirming a listing's Section 8 policy directly before you apply. The Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) administers the county's Housing Choice Voucher program, including for renters searching near East Cleveland, and its [preliminary application](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) stays open year-round with placement by rolling lottery rather than strict first-come-first-served. Every home we work with nearby accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready — see our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full picture.

Because East Cleveland is a separate municipality from the City of Cleveland, electric service here typically comes from The Illuminating Company (FirstEnergy) rather than the city-owned Cleveland Public Power utility, which mainly serves addresses inside Cleveland's city limits. Natural gas is generally supplied by Enbridge Gas Ohio (formerly Dominion East Ohio / The East Ohio Gas Company), and Cleveland Water — one of the largest municipal water systems in the country — serves East Cleveland along with the city and many other suburbs. Confirm the exact provider for a specific address before signing a lease.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in East Cleveland?
faq.a: Some are, but Ohio law doesn't require it and East Cleveland hasn't passed its own source-of-income protection ordinance, so acceptance varies by landlord. CMHA administers the county's Housing Choice Voucher program and can help you search. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with nearby accepts vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready — contact us to ask what's close to East Cleveland.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in East Cleveland?
faq.a: Dedicated East Cleveland rent data isn't published separately, so the closest benchmark is the wider Cleveland market. Zumper's July 2026 report puts the area median around $1,250 a month, while RentCafe's professionally-managed-apartment average runs higher, near $1,564. An individual East Cleveland house's asking rent depends on its size, age, and condition.

The RTA Red Line's Windermere station is East Cleveland's eastern terminus, giving a direct rail connection to downtown Cleveland's Tower City and to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport. The HealthLine bus rapid transit route along Euclid Avenue also connects East Cleveland to University Circle and Public Square without needing a transfer.

faq.q: Is East Cleveland part of the city of Cleveland?
faq.a: No. East Cleveland is its own incorporated city in Cuyahoga County with its own government and services, bordering Cleveland's University Circle neighborhood along Euclid Avenue. It's often grouped with Cleveland in casual conversation, regional transit maps, and housing searches because the two are directly adjacent and share the same RTA and HealthLine transit lines.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with homes in East Cleveland?
faq.a: Our current selection is concentrated in Cleveland proper, particularly the east and southeast side. We don't have confirmed inventory inside East Cleveland itself, but we work with voucher-friendly homes nearby in neighborhoods like Glenville and Collinwood — contact us and we'll let you know what's available close by.
